edus.text: Former New York City mayor Mike Bloomberg , a centrist billionaire
who launched a White House bid
after years of flirting with a run ,
and pledged to use his vast resources
to unite Democrats and moderate Republicans against President Donald Trump
has suspended his campaign
and is endorsing former Vice President Joe Biden .
His campaign was shaken by Biden ' s resurgence and overwhelming victory in the South Carolina primary ,
which reignited the former vice president 's bid
and led other moderate candidates to drop out of the race end endorse him in an effort
to consolidate the primary vote
and prevent Vermont Sen. Bernie Sanders from clinching the party ' s nomination .
By Super Tuesday , it was clear that Bloomberg 's efforts
to target the 15 contests in play in lieu of traditional campaigning methods
of hitting the early voting states
simply did not pay off in terms
of netting the bulk of pledged delegates or the popular vote .
" Three months ago , I entered the race for President
to defeat Donald Trump .
Today , I am leaving the race for the same reason :
to defeat Donald Trump
- because it is clear to me
that staying in would make achieving that goal more difficult , ''
he wrote in a letter to supporters
adding
" I ' ve always believed
that defeating Donald Trump starts with uniting behind the candidate with the best shot
to do it .
After yesterday ' s vote , it is clear
that candidate is my friend and a great American , Joe Biden . ''
Biden ,
who has acknowledged his lackluster ground game ,
told
that changes were coming to his campaign in terms of staffing .
On Wednesday , he tweeted his thanks to Bloomberg
whose endorsement could possibly help strengthen the former vice president 's bid with a potential huge influx of cash and organization assistance .
`` @ MikeBloomberg , I can ' t thank you enough for your support
and for your tireless work on everything from gun safety reform to climate change .
his race is bigger than candidates
nd bigger than politics .
t ' s about defeating Donald Trump ,
nd with your help , we ' re gon na do it , "
iden tweeted .
emocratic presidential candidate Michael Bloomberg addresses supporters at his Super Tuesday night rally in West Palm Beach , Fla. , March 3 , 2020 .
arco Bello/Reuters
espite ruling out a White House run last spring ,
loomberg reversed course in the fall
nd entered the race just before Thanksgiving ,
ffering himself as a pragmatic problem solver with unlimited resources
ho could appeal to the middle of the country
lienated by Trump and leading progressives , at a moment
hen former vice president Joe Biden faced questions about the strength of his campaign .
loomberg ' s campaign surged after Biden ' s disastrous fourth-place finish in the Iowa caucuses ,
mpressing voters and Democratic officials with the size and scale of his operation
espite skipping the first four early primary contests ,
nd seizing on concerns
hat Sanders ' nomination could endanger down-ballot Democrats across the country this fall .
` Defeating Trump
and rebuilding America -
s the most urgent and important fight of our lives .
nd I ' m going all in , "
loomberg said on his website at the time .
n the first 100 days of his campaign , Bloomberg spent more than half a billion dollars of his estimated $ 60 billion fortune on his campaign ,
looding airwaves around the country with ads ,
pening hundreds of offices across the country ,
nd hiring more than 2,100 people to his team ,
uilding an operation from scratch
hat resembled a general election operation more than a primary campaign .
is was a novel gambit :
hrowing out the traditional primary rulebook altogether ,
ypassing the four early states altogether
nd focusing in on delegate-rich Super Tuesday spots like Texas , California and Florida .
oreover , he specifically homed in on the swing states
rump picked up in 2016
hat Democrats " should " have won , like Ohio or Michigan .
loomberg ' s energetic efforts at outreach demonstrate an immense appetite
or seeking voters
here they live ,
nd showing his commitment ,
specially visiting those areas off the beaten campaign trail during the early primary stages ;
s other candidates campaigned in Iowa with a weather eye towards March -
loomberg was already all over the map .
oreover , he swiftly assembled a formidable ground game :
ield staff swelling to over 2,000 in 43 states and territories ,
ncluding all Super Tuesday states ;
ore than 400 in their headquarters .
n his massive operation , Bloomberg called it an " investment "
n beating Trump .
Some of these candidates criticize me
or spending a lot of money , "
loomberg said at a December stump stop in Ohio .
I think
you want me to spend less
o get rid of Donald Trump ?
don ' t think so . "
loomberg ' s campaign has said
t would " spend whatever it takes "
o remove Trump from office ;
e has left open the possibility
hat he could eventually put as much as a billion dollars into the race
even if it 's not his to run -
ledging to keep his operation open
ven if he is not the nominee -
o help whoever does eventually win .
ermont Sen. Bernie Sanders said
e would refuse Bloomberg ' s help ,
hould he win the nomination ;
en. Elizabeth Warren , D-Mass. ,
hough she said
he ' d accept Bloomberg ' s aid ,
lso did not hold back :
n turns accusing him of " buying his way " into the election ,
nd that he was " skipping the democracy part " of the primary .
e faced sharp rebuke from rivals
ho criticized his large ad buys and campaign strategy .
espite slick commercials
hat portrayed Bloomberg as an effective and decisive manager
ho rebuilt New York City after the September 11th terror attacks ,
loomberg , an uneven debater and retail politician , never recovered from a poor showing in his first primary debate ,
hen he struggled to respond to attacks about his past as a Republican , his wealth and a series of nondisclosure agreements
elated to alleged comments towards women at his company ,
long with the controversial stop-and-frisk policing tactics
sed in the city during his tenure .
ust before announcing his 2020 bid ,
loomberg made a very public apology for his support of stop and frisk
and continued to do so over the course of his campaign ,
ouring through black and brown communities ,
eeting with leaders ,
nd rolling out several criminal justice and economic policies
pecifically geared towards lifting up those communities most hurt by such divisive issues .
is past treatment of women in his employ , as well as the culture at his eponymous company , has also faced intense scrutiny .
breaking investigation in December explored the repeated occasions
here Bloomberg had faced and fought allegations
hat he directed crude and sexist comments to women in his office ,
ncluding a claim in the 1990s
hat he told an employee
ho had just announced
he was pregnant
o `` kill it . ''
loomberg has repeatedly denied making these and other comments .
he nondisclosure agreements
e has kept with many of his employees
eep any of those on the receiving end of these comments from sharing their side .
n the debate stage , Warren tore into Bloomberg ,
emanding
s she had told for months
hat he release the women from their secrecy agreements .
or his part , Bloomberg just days later announced
hat he would release three of those signatories from their agreement .
